text:
Once upon an ancient time in a forest inhabited by creatures ancient beyond counting, there lived a wise old owl. Every morning she would flap her wings gently towards the dew-covered maple leaves.

One particular morning, she flew higher than usual. As she soared lower, something glimmered among the clouds. Intrigued, she came down. It was a glittering starfall. She watched stars come and go.

Days turned into weeks. She discovered she could talk to the stars.

"You seek wisdom," breathed a particular star. "Ask anything."

"My heart aches for something I can't have," she sighed.

"Why do souls long missing?"

She pondered. "Perhaps because I've forgotten my own joy."

"In nightfall," twinkled another star. "You'll find your own light."

As months passed, evenings became her favorite time. She listened. Whispers flew.

Suddenly, she realized her own voice.

"The forest has many gifts," she said confidently. The animals listened.
